title: tick_error_timeout() uses the egui repaint loop as a clock to auto-dismiss errors without a timer thread
description: tick_error_timeout() is called once per frame in update(). It records Instant::now() when an error is set and clears it after 5 elapsed seconds. This uses egui's own repaint cadence as the timing mechanism — no separate timer thread, no OS timer, no tokio interval task needed. The alternatives are worse: a timer thread adds a third thread for a cosmetic concern; a tokio interval in client_task would require adding a new AppEvent variant and complicating the task loop for a purely UI concern; leaving errors visible forever is worse UX. A contributor might question why error dismissal is not driven by the background task. That would be wrong: error display lifetime is a UI policy, not a network policy. The pattern is correct: UI timing belongs in UI code, driven by the UI's own clock (the frame loop).
